purpose: Compact decision map for post-Batch-1 service-system consolidation and Batch 2 planning. Use this to decide what to keep, de-emphasize, merge, or build next without rereading the full planning stack.

keepIf: the page maps cleanly to a current runtime cluster
the page represents a commercially clear offer
the slug is buyer-readable and aligned with current strategy
the page adds specificity that Batch 1 pages do not already cover
deemphasizeIf: the page overlaps heavily with a stronger Batch 1 page
the naming is legacy or less buyer-readable than the new system
the page is still valid but should not be surfaced as a featured child right now
mergeOrRetireIf: the page duplicates another page's core job
the slug reflects an older architecture no longer used in runtime
the offer is real but better expressed as part of a stronger parent or sibling page

candidates.why: Strong differentiator for the current brand and fits the systems/AI direction without overlapping too heavily with crm-architecture.
candidates.notes: Should be framed as applied internal systems and automation, not vague AI language.

candidates.why: Commercially clear entry service that supports consulting-led engagement and pairs well with the contact flow.
candidates.notes: Should stay diagnostic and practical, not read like a generic audit checklist.

candidates.why: Strategically relevant, but currently more specialty and less essential than the Batch 1 pages or agentic systems / martech audit.
candidates.notes: Likely better as a specialty page or nested growth page after visual support and core system cleanup.

candidates.why: Still conceptually relevant, but naming is more branded/internal than buyer-readable compared with the current service system.
candidates.notes: May survive as a branded framework inside another page rather than as a front-facing child page.

candidates.why: Still a real service, but currently overlaps with website-strategy and older architecture. Not the best next commercial page to emphasize.
candidates.notes: Could later return as a narrower messaging/brand layer if the system needs it.

candidates.why: Too close to website-strategy and conversion-optimization. The new service system already expresses this more cleanly through two stronger pages.
candidates.notes: Should not be re-elevated as a featured child while the new system is stabilizing.

2026-04-01: `martech-audit` Batch 2 page 1 implemented in `data/services.ts` + doc updates; next build `agentic-marketing-systems`.

2026-04-01: `geo-optimization` Batch 2 page 3 implemented — nested `/services/growth/geo-optimization`; suggested Batch 2 trio complete — next: consolidation check / proof-media cleanup / light services polish.

2026-04-01: Post–Batch 2 refinement — `parent-child-linking-kit.json` updated for `geo-optimization`; `batch2Status` complete; legacy `secondaryCtaHref` cleanup + `/tools/graston-growth-engine` redirect — see CLAUDE.md.

2026-04-02: Custom tools / workflow-products direction documented (definition-only) — `custom-tools-service-priority-map.json`, `custom-tools-service-plan.md`, `custom-tools-proof-candidates.json`; preferred fit = specialty nested under systems cluster; no Batch 1/Batch 2 page rewrites; runtime implementation deferred — see CLAUDE.md.
